states.name: Alabama
states.summary: Alabama offers tax incentives for data center development, including sales tax exemptions on qualifying equipment and construction materials.

states.name: Alaska
states.summary: No known data center moratorium or incentive legislation.

states.name: Arizona
states.summary: Arizona has become a major data center hub with state-level tax incentives. Cities like Mesa and Goodyear actively recruit hyperscale facilities.

states.name: Arkansas
states.summary: Arkansas provides tax incentives for data center investment, including reduced sales tax on qualifying equipment purchases.

states.name: California
states.summary: No statewide moratorium legislation, though some local jurisdictions have imposed water-use restrictions affecting proposed data center sites.

states.name: Colorado
states.summary: Colorado legislators are reviewing data center energy consumption standards and considering requiring renewable energy commitments for new facilities.

states.name: Connecticut
states.summary: Connecticut is reviewing the environmental and grid impacts of proposed data center developments, with committee hearings scheduled.

states.name: Delaware
states.summary: No known data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: Florida
states.summary: No statewide data center moratorium legislation. Florida continues to attract data center investment, particularly in the Miami and Jacksonville markets.

states.name: Georgia
states.summary: Georgia introduced HB 1012, proposing a ban on new hyperscale data center construction until March 2027 to study grid capacity and water impacts.

states.name: Hawaii
states.summary: No known data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: Idaho
states.summary: Idaho offers tax exemptions for data center equipment and has seen growth in data center construction, particularly in the Boise metro area.

states.name: Illinois
states.summary: Illinois legislators are examining data center energy usage amid concerns about grid reliability, with hearings in the energy committee.

states.name: Indiana
states.summary: Indiana introduced HB 1333 taking a revenue-sharing approach, requiring data centers to contribute to local infrastructure rather than imposing a moratorium.

states.name: Iowa
states.summary: Iowa provides significant tax incentives for data center investment and has attracted major hyperscale facilities from Meta, Microsoft, and Google.

states.name: Kansas
states.summary: Kansas offers property tax exemptions and sales tax incentives for qualifying data center investments.

states.name: Kentucky
states.summary: Kentucky provides tax incentives for data center development, including sales tax exemptions on qualifying equipment.

states.name: Louisiana
states.summary: Louisiana offers the Digital Interactive Media and Software Tax Credit and other incentives favorable to data center construction.

states.name: Maine
states.summary: Maine introduced LD 307, proposing a ban on data centers over 20 MW until November 2027 to assess environmental and grid impacts.

bills.title: Act to Impose a Moratorium on Large-Scale Data Centers

states.name: Maryland
states.summary: Maryland introduced HB 120, proposing a moratorium on new data center construction until co-location and grid reliability legislation is enacted.

bills.title: Data Center Moratorium and Grid Reliability Act

states.name: Massachusetts
states.summary: Massachusetts is reviewing energy and water consumption requirements for data centers, with committee discussions ongoing.

states.name: Michigan
states.summary: Michigan has introduced legislation to study data center impacts on the electrical grid and consider temporary construction restrictions.

bills.title: Data Center Grid Impact Study Act

states.name: Minnesota
states.summary: Minnesota introduced SF 4298, proposing a freeze on new data center construction until the Public Utilities Commission completes a grid impact review.

states.name: Mississippi
states.summary: Mississippi offers tax incentives to attract data center development, including property tax exemptions for qualifying facilities.

states.name: Missouri
states.summary: Missouri provides sales tax exemptions for data center equipment and construction materials for qualifying facilities.

states.name: Montana
states.summary: Montana has attracted data center interest due to low energy costs and cool climate, with state incentives supporting development.

states.name: Nebraska
states.summary: Nebraska offers significant tax incentives for data center development, including the ImagiNE Nebraska Act benefits.

states.name: Nevada
states.summary: Nevada, particularly the Reno-Sparks area, is a major data center market with state tax abatements for qualifying facilities.

states.name: New Hampshire
states.summary: New Hampshire introduced HB 1265, proposing a one-year freeze on new data center construction to study energy and environmental impacts.

states.name: New Jersey
states.summary: New Jersey is reviewing data center impacts on the electrical grid and water resources, with legislative hearings underway.

states.name: New Mexico
states.summary: No known statewide data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: New York
states.summary: New York has proposed S9144, a 3-year moratorium on new data center construction to study environmental and grid impacts. The state previously passed a 2-year moratorium on proof-of-work crypto mining facilities in 2022.

states.name: North Carolina
states.summary: North Carolina is reviewing data center energy demands as rapid growth in the Charlotte and Triangle regions strains the electrical grid.

states.name: North Dakota
states.summary: North Dakota offers tax incentives and low energy costs that attract data center development.

states.name: Ohio
states.summary: Ohio legislators are reviewing the impact of large-scale data center development on the electrical grid and local communities.

states.name: Oklahoma
states.summary: Oklahoma introduced SB 1488, proposing a moratorium on new data center construction until November 2029 to study water and energy impacts.

states.name: Oregon
states.summary: Oregon is reviewing data center water usage and environmental requirements, particularly in the Dalles region where Google operates large facilities.

states.name: Pennsylvania
states.summary: Pennsylvania has introduced legislation to study data center grid impacts and consider temporary restrictions on new construction near nuclear facilities.

bills.title: Data Center and Grid Reliability Study Act

states.name: Rhode Island
states.summary: No known statewide data center moratorium or restriction legislation.

states.name: South Carolina
states.summary: South Carolina introduced H. 5286, proposing a freeze on new data center construction until January 2028 to assess grid and water impacts.

states.name: South Dakota
states.summary: South Dakota introduced SB 232, proposing a one-year moratorium on hyperscale data center construction to study infrastructure impacts.

states.name: Tennessee
states.summary: Tennessee is reviewing data center tax incentive programs and energy consumption impacts, with committee discussions ongoing.

states.name: Texas
states.summary: Texas is one of the largest data center markets in the US with Chapter 313 and successor incentive programs. Dallas-Fort Worth and San Antonio are major hubs.

states.name: Utah
states.summary: Utah offers tax incentives for data center development and has seen significant growth in the Salt Lake City area.

states.name: Vermont
states.summary: Vermont has proposed legislation for a freeze on data center construction until July 2030, one of the longest proposed moratorium periods in the country.

states.name: Virginia
states.summary: Virginia introduced HB 1515, proposing a moratorium on new data center construction until July 2028. Virginia is the largest data center market in the world, concentrated in Northern Virginia's Data Center Alley.

states.name: Washington
states.summary: Washington state is reviewing data center energy and water consumption, particularly in areas served by hydroelectric power in central Washington.

states.name: West Virginia
states.summary: West Virginia offers tax incentives for data center development and is marketing its low energy costs to attract facilities.

states.name: Wisconsin
states.summary: Wisconsin introduced LRB-6377, requiring data centers to meet environmental protection standards and community benefit agreements before construction.

states.name: Wyoming
states.summary: Wyoming offers low energy costs and tax incentives favorable to data center development.
